notes: Dwell time MedD -3.9h (95%CI -23.7 to 15.9). PIVC failure includes infiltration/occlusion/dislodgement. Total device time median 2.0 vs 2.0 days. Primary outcome was first-attempt insertion success (not post-insertion).

PIVC lifespan = time from insertion to removal (max 3 days protocol). Extravasation trended higher in USG group. Catheters tended larger in USG (18G vs 21G). Null result for primary outcome (number of attempts).

outcome_dwell_time_usg: KM survival probabilities reported at multiple timepoints
outcome_dwell_time_lm: KM survival probabilities reported at multiple timepoints
outcome_infection_lm: KM at 24h: USG 0.82 vs PIV 0.86; at 48h: 0.70 vs 0.70; at 72h: 0.61 vs 0.57; at 96h: 0.52 vs 0.46; at 120h: 0.47 vs 0.38. USG slightly worse at 24h but better long-term. All-cause removal similar. Adjusted HR favors USGPIV.

outcome_failure_usg: HR 2.20 (1.45-3.34) traditional vs USG (unadjusted); age-adjusted HR 1.99 (1.28-3.08)
rob_tool: MAJOR CONFOUNDING: USG group exclusively used longer catheters (4.4cm). Also USG patients significantly older. Additional PIV access needed: OR 0.39 (0.18-0.85) p=0.017 favoring USG. 3-day IV survival 59.5% vs 83.0%.

fulltext_available: Null result. USG catheters more likely to be long needle (1 7/8in). USG more often placed above elbow. Premature removal mainly due to pain/discomfort or catheter dysfunction. No significant KM survival difference.

outcome_patency_2hr_usg: Only outcome: contrast extravasation. USG catheter CE rate 4.1% vs standard 0.21%. Result unfavorable to USG. Small number of CE events (n=74 total). No dwell time data.

outcome_patency_2hr_p: Catheter patency at 2 hours is only post-insertion outcome. Complications: hematoma USG 2(4.44%) vs LM 6(13.33%) p=0.14; arterial puncture USG 0 vs LM 1(2.22%); nerve irritation: 0 both groups. Short follow-up (2 hours only).

fulltext_available: Null result across all post-insertion outcomes. Underpowered (target 582, enrolled 223 due to COVID). CVR cutoff ~0.21 (AUC 0.621) not predictive. Postremoval complications: redness/warmth, tenderness/pain, edema/swelling.

catheter_type_confounding: YES (USG=long 8cm polyurethane LC vs blind=short <6cm Teflon SC)
notes: MAJOR CONFOUNDING: USG=long polyurethane cannulas (8cm Seldinger) vs blind=short Teflon cannulas. Different catheter material and length. Episodes of dislocation/infiltration/occlusion/thrombosis: LC 0.3±0.4 vs SC 0.7±0.5 (p=0.003). Catheters changed during therapy: LC 1.2±0.3 vs SC 2.8±1.0. Two venous thromboses in LC group.
Conference abstract only. Null result. Logistic regression: no significant association between technique and infiltration. 12 infiltration events with unclear technique excluded.
